meaning: adj.苏格兰的植物学家Robert Brown 的
examples: The increase of internal energy (temperature rise) results from the molecule Brownian Movement.
The idea is that share prices follow some gentle random walk away from an equilibrium, rather like motes of dust jiggling around in Brownian motion.
Raizen's study now proves that the equipartition theorem is true for Brownian particles; in this case, glass beads that were three micrometers across.
He says financial models built on Brownian motion have done a great deal of good.
Similarly, it appears that the limits to our threshold of hearing may actually be Brownian motion.
